title: MaintenanceSchedule
description: The WHEN & WHERE that turns a plan applied to an asset into generated work: it binds a job plan (or class-family plan, at a chosen level) to one asset, anchors the cadence in time, and holds the per-frequency high-water marks that keep generation IDEMPOTENT. In the serverless/file-based deployment, PM work is generated lazily when the app opens; the high-water marks are the state that stops the same due date being generated twice. One JSON document = one schedule (one plan applied to one asset).

assetId.description: The asset this schedule generates work for.

jobPlanId.description: The plan applied — a jobPlan or assetClassPlan id.

planLevel.description: For a class-family plan, which level's resolved effective plan to snapshot (class base, a model variant, or an instance). Ignored for a single job plan.

anchorDate.description: The date the cadence is measured from (commissioning / last-done). The k-th occurrence of a frequency falls at anchorDate + k x (interval x unit); the first due is one interval after the anchor.

mergeWindowDays.description: Nesting (cannibalism) window: frequencies whose due dates fall within this many days of each other collapse into ONE work order — the longest-interval frequency drives it and absorbs the shorter ones (the de-duplicated union of their tasks). 0 = merge only exact-coincident due dates.

leadTimeDays.description: How far ahead of the due date a work order is generated, so planning/parts have runway. Generation covers due dates up to (asOf + leadTimeDays).

priority.description: Priority stamped on generated work orders. Combines with asset criticality downstream.

responsibleTeamId.description: Default team generated work is assigned to.

workflowProfileKey.description: Workflow profile generated orders follow; null = the org default.

active.description: When false, no work is generated (the schedule is suspended without losing its high-water marks).

highWaterMarks.description: Per-frequency generation cursor: frequencyId -> the ISO date of the last due date already generated (or covered by a merge). Generation only emits due dates strictly after the mark, then advances it. Absent frequency = never generated yet (start from the anchor).

rev.description: Optimistic-concurrency revision; bumped on every save.
